title: The magic of a long runway
intro: Eicher Motors was about ₹46 crore at the end of FY01. Feel what two decades of compounding does.
prompt: If a ₹46 crore business compounded its value at ~25% a year for 20 years, roughly what would it be worth (₹ crore)?
debrief: ₹46 cr × 1.25^20 ≈ ₹3,988 cr — an ~87x rise, from one unremarkable starting number. That is why the durable compounders looked so ordinary early: the outcome lives in the exponent (rate × years), which our intuition badly underestimates. Sustained earnings growth over a long runway is the whole game.
